channel.title: João A. S. Amarante
channel.description: Recent content on João A. S. Amarante

item.title: MWBR Journal Club
item.description:
March 25th 2026:

  • Thayse: Chemo-dynamical reconstruction of Milky Way globular cluster progenitors: age–metallicity relations and the universality of multiple stellar populations
  • Guilherme: An Ultra-Faint, Chemically Primitive Galaxy Forming in the Reionization Era

Martch 18th 2026:

  • André: Identifying ∼20,000 Li-rich Giants in the LAMOST Low-resolution Survey

March 11th 2026:

  • Lais: Scattering, Migration, Re-circularization and Relaxation to Build Out Galaxy Disks with Exponential Profiles

March 4th 2026:

  • Guilherme: A Chemodynamical Census of the Milky Way’s Ultra-Faint Compact Satellites. I. A First Population-Level Look at the Internal Kinematics and Metallicities of 19 Extremely-Low-Mass Halo Stellar Systems
  • Ingrid: A Vertically Orientated Dark Matter Halo Marks a Flip of the Galactic Disk

February 11th 2026:

  • Guilherme: Proof that the Milky Way experienced a significant merger only 1.5 billion years after the Big Bang
  • João: Galaxy archaeology for wet mergers: Globular cluster age distributions in the Milky Way and nearby galaxies

February 04th 2026:

  • Nayara: The Milky Way’s circular velocity curve measured using element abundance gradients
  • Otavio: The origin of strong α-element bimodalities in FIRE simulations of Milky Way-mass galaxies

January 28th 2026:

  • Joao: Ancient relic moderately metal-rich bulge cluster Tonantzintla 2
  • Hélio: That’s so Retro: The Gaia-Sausage-Enceladus Merger Trajectory as the Origin of the Chemical Abundance Bimodality in the Milky Way Disk

January 21st 2026:

  • Lais: Early thin-disc assembly revealed by JWST edge-on galaxies
  • Guilherme: A Possible “Too-Many-Satellites” Problem in the Isolated Dwarf Galaxy DDO 161

December 17th 2025:

  • Hélio: Impact of selection criteria on the structural parameters of the Galactic thin and thick discs

December 3rd 2025:

  • Lais: An Indication of Gas Inflow in Clumpy Star-Forming Galaxies near z~1: Lower Gas-Phase Metallicities in Clumpy Galaxies Compared to Non-Clumpy Galaxies

5-min self-authorship talks:

item.title: Victor Debattista's Galaxy Dynamics group Journal Club
item.description:
March 10th 2026:

  • Benjamin: What drives bar rotation? The effect of internal properties and galaxy interactions on bar pattern speeds

March 3rd 2026:

  • Dave: Bar formation in disc galaxies: internal kinematics and environmental influence in MaNGA galaxies

  • Joao: Two faces of Gaia-Sausage-Enceladus: Mining the chemical abundance space with graph attention networks

  • Micole: Stirring Things Up: Bar-induced substructures in the stellar halo of a cosmological Milky Way analogue

  • Mika: Signatures of a Tidally Induced Spiral Arm at the Anticenter of the Milky Way and a Kinematically Extended Anticenter Stream Using DESI DR2

  • Xiaojie: Galactic bars are already mature at Cosmic Noon: bar strength and flatness at z ∼ 1.5

January 20th 2026:

  • Benjamin: Stellar Bars in Jellyfish Galaxies: Statistical Insights into the Combined Role of Bars and Environment
